notes: Anglerfish ambush firefly squid drawn toward their bioluminescent lure in overlapping depth ranges.
Anglerfish are known to consume barreleye fish that stray into deeper water during vertical migrations.
Frilled sharks prey on crustaceans and slow-moving invertebrates including juvenile isopods encountered on the continental slope.
Vampire squid are believed to be occasional prey for frilled sharks patrolling the upper midnight zone.
Goblin sharks forage along the seafloor and consume sea pigs encountered during benthic cruising.
Giant isopods are targeted by goblin sharks whose jaw-slingshot mechanism bypasses their armored exoskeleton.
Stoplight loosejaw use their invisible red bioluminescence to illuminate and ambush firefly squid undetected.
Loosejaws consume barreleye fish by opening their unhinged jaw wider than the prey's body diameter.
Vampire squid scavenge detritus but have been observed capturing small squid including juvenile firefly squid.
Ping-pong tree sponge hooks snare small crustaceans; juvenile isopods have been found entangled in specimens.
Siphonophores trail long tentacles that ensnare small squid and fish including firefly squid in the twilight zone.
Siphonophore nematocysts can paralyse small fish like the barreleye that swim into their trailing tentacle curtain.
